title: Centaurea micevskii
intro: Centaurea micevskii, also known as Macedonian knapweed, is a perennial plant species belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae). The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various conditions such as wounds, infections, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Centaurea micevskii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often employed in folk medicine to support digestive health and alleviate symptoms of fever and inflammation.
taxonomy: Centaurea micevskii is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Centaurea, commonly known as knapweed.
